[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭}} held at {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival is held on the first {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} and {{ill|Sunday|ja|日曜日}} of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|lt=Deity of rice fields|ja|田の神}}, are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Shrine approach|ja|参道}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

revision.text:
[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭}} held at {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival is held on the first {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} and {{ill|Sunday|ja|日曜日}} of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|lt=Deity of rice fields|ja|田の神}}, are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Shrine approach|ja|参道}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.
== References == * *{{Cite book|ja|author=Year-round Event Encyclopedia|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2009|isbn=978-4-642-01443-4}}
== External Links == * [https://katori-jingu.or.jp/2017/03/20/4%e6%9c%88%e3%81%ae%e7%a5%ad%e5%85%b8%e3%81%a8%e5%82%ac%e3%81%97/ Official Website of Katori Shrine - April Festivals and Events]
{{Event-stub}}

Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Shrine approach|ja|参道|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=draft}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft}} held at {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival is held on the first {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sunday|ja|日曜日|12=draft}} of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=draft}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=draft}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=draft}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|lt=Deity of rice fields|ja|田の神|12=draft}}, are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Shrine approach|ja|参道|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=draft}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|香取神宮御田植祭]] '''香取神宮御田植祭'''（かとりじんぐうおたうえさい）は、[[千葉県]][[香取市]]香取の[[香取神宮]]で催される[[御田植祭]]である。
== 概要 == 香取神宮の御田植祭は、4月の第一[[土曜日|土曜]]と[[日曜日|日曜]]に行われ、1日目の「耕田式」と2日目の「田植式」からなる。
;耕田式 御田植祭の1日目は拝殿前で「耕田式」が行われる。苗長を先頭に、牛方が五色絹に彩られた[[牛]]の耕機をつけて牽き、その後ろから[[緋]]の[[袴]]をつけ[[鎌]]を手にした[[少女]]、[[直垂]]を着け[[鋤]]を手にした[[男]]、同じく[[鍬]]を持った男、そして白衣緋袴に模様の入った[[襷]]をかけ背に[[花笠]]を背負った田舞の少女8人、華傘をさしかけられ[[肩車]]された[[田の神#早乙女|早乙女]]役の[[稚児]]8人が、庭上に入る。鎌入れの所作、鋤入れ・鍬入れの所作に続き、牛により代掻きが行われ、少女8人が苗長から受け取った[[早苗]]を手に田舞をし、年配者の早乙女手代による[[田植え]]の所作をもって「耕田式」は終わる。
;田植式 2日目には「田植式」が行われる。前日と同様、拝殿前にて露払・鍬取・老姥や華傘の稚児が庭上へ進み、少女の田舞の後、[[田植歌|田植え歌]]によって早乙女手代が田植えの所作を行う。拝殿前の[[神事]]が終わると[[参道]]から[[御田|斎田]]へと向かい、献餞、祝詞奏上などの神事が執り行われた後、早乙女手代が田植え歌を唄いながら斎田の田植えをする。
== 参考文献 == * 『年中行事大辞典』（吉川弘文館、2009年）ISBN 978-4-642-01443-4
== 外部リンク == *[https://katori-jingu.or.jp/2017/03/20/4%e6%9c%88%e3%81%ae%e7%a5%ad%e5%85%b8%e3%81%a8%e5%82%ac%e3%81%97/ 香取神宮のホームページ ４月の祭典と催し]
{{Event-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かとりしんくうおたうえさい}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:香取市の文化]] [[Category:予祝芸能]] [[Category:稲作]]

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.
== References == * *{{Cite book|ja|author=Year-round Event Encyclopedia|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2009|isbn=978-4-642-01443-4}}
== External Links == * [https://katori-jingu.or.jp/2017/03/20/4%e6%9c%88%e3%81%ae%e7%a5%ad%e5%85%b8%e3%81%a8%e5%82%ac%e3%81%97/ Official Website of Katori Shrine - April Festivals and Events]
{{Event-stub}}

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11667185]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival]] '''Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Katori Shrine Rice-Planting Festival is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."

Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. [[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]]
[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] in [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first [[:en:Saturday|Saturday]] and [[:en:Sunday|Sunday]] of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Deity of rice fields]], are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the [[:en:Sandō|Shrine approach]] to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:香取神宮御田植祭]]
[[File:Rice-transplanting Festival in Katori-jingu 1,katori-city,japan.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū]] '''Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū''' (香取神宮御田植祭, Katori Jingū Otaue-sai) is a {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Rice-planting festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice-planting festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == The Otaue Festival at Katori Jingū is held on the first {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|WD=Q131|lt=Saturday}} and {{ill|Sunday|en|Sunday|WD=Q132|lt=Sunday}} of April. It consists of two parts: the "Plowing Ceremony" on the first day and the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" on the second day.
; Plowing Ceremony On the first day of the festival, the "Plowing Ceremony" takes place in front of the worship hall. Leading the procession is the head of seedlings, followed by ox handlers guiding oxen adorned with {{ill|Five-colored silk|ja|五色絹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-colored silk|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} yoked to a plow. Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Deity of rice fields}}, are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=Shrine approach}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.

Behind them are girls wearing {{ill|Scarlet hakama|ja|緋の袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scarlet hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, carrying sickles; men in {{ill|Hitatare|ja|直垂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitatare|qq=}}, holding plows and hoes; and eight girls dressed in white with scarlet hakama, wearing floral sashes and carrying flower hats on their backs. Additionally, eight children in ceremonial attire, representing the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=Deity of rice fields|1=Ta-no-Kami}}, are carried on shoulders under floral umbrellas. The ceremony includes symbolic actions of inserting sickles, plows, and hoes, followed by plowing by the oxen. The eight girls then perform a ritual dance with seedlings handed to them by the head of seedlings, and the older rice-planting assistants carry out planting gestures, concluding the "Plowing Ceremony."
; Rice-Planting Ceremony On the second day, the "Rice-Planting Ceremony" is performed. Similar to the previous day, participants such as ceremonial sweepers, hoe handlers, elderly women, and children under ceremonial umbrellas gather in front of the worship hall. After a ritual dance by the girls, accompanied by rice-planting songs, the rice-planting assistants perform symbolic planting actions. Following the rituals at the worship hall, the participants proceed from the {{ill|Sandō|en|Sandō|WD=Q667783|lt=Shrine approach|1=Sandō}} to the {{ill|Sacred rice field|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, where further ceremonies, including offerings and prayer recitals, are conducted. The rice-planting assistants then plant rice in the sacred field while singing traditional rice-planting songs.
